description_long: This object is a stringed musical instrument with a teardrop-shaped wooden body and a long, straight neck. The soundboard features a decorative circular rosette at its center, and the instrument has multiple strings running from the bridge to the headstock. The back of the body is rounded and appears to be made from a single piece of wood, giving it a robust, resonant chamber. The headstock is angled and fitted with several tuning pegs arranged in two rows, suggesting a historical or classical design, possibly a lute. The surface shows signs of wear and aging, with a matte, slightly rough finish and muted, earthy tones.
description: A teardrop-shaped, wooden stringed instrument with a long neck, decorative rosette, and multiple tuning pegs, showing signs of age and use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the teardrop body, decorative rosette, strings, and full neck with tuning pegs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the instrument's deep, rounded back and the straight neck extending outward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth, curved back of the body and the headstock from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the instrument's depth and the profile of the neck and headstock.
attribute_headstock_design: Angled headstock with two rows of tuning pegs, no visible logo, classical/renaissance style.
